summary: Warbay is a done-for-you, AI-enhanced email marketing agency that grows revenue from a client's email list across four service pillars: data monetization, list management, marketing automation, and deliverability. It sells a monthly retainer to SMB-to-mid-market business owners and marketing leads who have (or want to build) an email list but lack the in-house expertise, bandwidth, or deliverability infrastructure to monetize it. The entire sales funnel gates on a single qualifying demo call.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get a newly-signed client from contract to their first live campaign (the 'aha' moment) fast, before slow-start doubt sets in.
campaigns.trigger: Retainer agreement signed / client account created. Send instantly.
campaigns.segment: New signed clients; branch by the lead service pillar they bought (deliverability, automation, list management, or data monetization).
campaigns.rationale: Warbay's #1 churn risk is clients who never fully onboard — they don't send their list assets, never reach the first-campaign aha, and cancel. A fast, asset-collecting welcome directly attacks that and sets up the 2–4 week path to first measurable revenue.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set the launch timeline expectation, and drive the single onboarding action that unblocks the first campaign send.
emails.subject: Welcome to Warbay — let's get your first campaign live in 2 weeks
emails.preview: One quick step today and we'll start auditing your list this week.
keyPoints: Reassure: we handle everything — your only job today is the 5-minute onboarding kit (ESP access + list + brand assets)
Set the timeline: list audit this week, strategy next, first live campaign within 2–4 weeks
Name the dedicated point of contact and invite a real reply — a human reads every one
Frame the aha: the moment your first Warbay campaign hits the inbox and beats your prior baseline

campaigns.name: Demo Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended qualifying demo calls and recover the ones that slip, since every retainer starts with one booked call.
campaigns.trigger: Demo booked (confirmation + prep instant); 24h-before and morning-of reminders; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone who books a qualifying demo via the 'Qualify for Demo' flow.
campaigns.rationale: Warbay's entire funnel gates on a single demo call with no self-serve fallback — every no-show is a lost retainer. Reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average, making this the fastest revenue-protecting flow to ship.

emails.purpose: Confirm the demo, lock it into the calendar, and prime the call's value so the prospect shows up ready to talk numbers.
emails.subject: You're booked — here's how to get the most from your Warbay demo
emails.preview: Add it to your calendar + bring these 3 numbers so we can map your ROI live.
keyPoints: Confirm date/time with one-click add-to-calendar and a no-friction reschedule link
Set expectations: a 30-minute working session, not a pitch — we'll diagnose your deliverability and list revenue gaps
Prep ask: bring your current list size, monthly email revenue, and biggest email frustration so we can map projected ROI on the call
Tease the payoff: leave with a clear read on what your list could be earning

campaigns.name: Post-Demo Consult-to-Retainer Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a qualified demo into a signed retainer by handling the ROI objection with a concrete, personalized plan.
campaigns.trigger: Demo call completed; runs in parallel with a soft expiry/urgency nudge if no decision within ~7 days.
campaigns.segment: Prospects who attended a demo but haven't signed; branch by company type (DTC/ecom, publisher, B2B SaaS, lead-gen).
campaigns.rationale: The #1 objection is 'I can't justify the retainer until I see the ROI' — so the email that turns a demo into a deal is a tailored proposal that quantifies the upside for THEIR list, not a generic follow-up.

emails.purpose: Same-day recap that delivers a custom revenue plan and projected ROI, directly answering 'is this worth the retainer?'
emails.subject: Your custom email revenue plan from today's call
emails.preview: Here's the inbox + revenue projection we mapped for your list.
emails.cta: Review your plan & book the kickoff
keyPoints: Recap the specific deliverability and list-monetization gaps surfaced on the call
Quantify the opportunity: projected revenue lift for their list size, framed against the retainer cost
Disarm the in-house objection: show the work and infrastructure their team would otherwise own
Low-friction close: one link to review the plan and lock a kickoff date this week

campaigns.name: Free Deliverability Audit Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move cold leads toward demo-readiness by giving them a real, gated value asset and nurturing on the deliverability pain.
campaigns.trigger: Download of a new lead magnet — a free email deliverability audit / 'spam-to-inbox' scorecard.
campaigns.segment: Leads who downloaded the audit but are below the demo-ready threshold; branch by their top stated pain (spam, idle list, no automation).
campaigns.rationale: Warbay has no top-of-funnel asset beyond the demo gate, so cold traffic has nowhere to go but a high-commitment call. A deliverability audit magnet captures earlier-stage interest and nurtures it toward the demo.

emails.purpose: Deliver the audit instantly and frame the real revenue problem hiding behind a deliverability score.
emails.subject: Your deliverability audit is here — and it explains the missing revenue
emails.preview: Most of your list never sees your emails. Here's what that's costing you.
keyPoints: Deliver the audit/scorecard immediately, no extra steps
Reframe the problem: poor inbox placement isn't a tech issue, it's lost revenue from a list you already paid to build
Give one quick win they can act on today (e.g., a single authentication or list-hygiene fix)
Plant the next step: when they want the full fix done for them, a qualifying demo is one click away

campaigns.name: Lapsed-Prospect Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate cold prospects — demo no-shows and audit downloaders who went quiet — and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no clicks/replies after a demo no-show or audit download.
campaigns.segment: Dormant prospects who never booked or never returned; keep separate from churned former clients.
campaigns.rationale: An early-stage agency can't afford to let hard-won leads go cold, and as an email agency Warbay's own list hygiene is part of the pitch — a clean win-back flow both recovers 5–15% of dead leads and walks the talk.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt re-engagement that reminds the lapsed prospect of the core value and gives one low-friction reason to come back.
emails.subject: Still leaving money in your email list?
emails.preview: Your list is probably still underperforming. Two minutes to change that.
emails.cta: See what your list could earn
keyPoints: Acknowledge the gap without guilt — 'we never finished the conversation'
Restate the core value: more inbox placement, more revenue, none of the in-house lift
Offer a fresh, lower-commitment entry point (re-run the free audit or grab a short call)
Single clear CTA; non-responders route to a final sunset email before suppression
